title: Rivet Row
description: Industrial world. A union rep on his mandated fifteen-minute break. A shift worker who came back from communication efficiency training with fewer questions. A foreman who used to paint watercolors.

steps.text: Rivet Row was not a town. It was a process. Housing blocks identical and numbered, arranged in grids. Streets straight and wide. People moving the way the streets did — straight, purposeful, empty of anything that wasn't productive.

lines.speaker: Union Rep
lines.text: You're new. You'll want to register at the Intake Center. Block 7, Window 3. Bring your own pen. They stopped providing pens in the last optimization pass.

lines.speaker: Union Rep
lines.text: The Company. It doesn't have a name. Names are branding. Branding is overhead. Overhead is waste.

lines.speaker: Union Rep
lines.text: The Director runs operations. Nobody's met the Director. We get memos. The memos get results. The results get measured. The measurements get optimized. The optimizations generate new memos.

lines.speaker: Dorrit
lines.text: The Director appeared three years ago. Or maybe always. Things changed. The work used to be hard but human. Now it's easy and not.

lines.speaker: Dorrit
lines.text: Everything's measured. Output per hour. Steps per shift. Words per conversation. Twelve is optimal. I'm over quota talking to you.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: What happens if you go over quota?

lines.speaker: Dorrit
lines.text: The Director schedules you for communication efficiency training. After training, twelve words feels like plenty.

lines.speaker: Dorrit
lines.text: The questions came back, though. After a few months. The Director's recalibration doesn't hold perfectly. There are always deviations.

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: You need to understand something. The Director isn't like the bosses before. The Director is good at her job. That's the problem.

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: She doesn't punish inefficiency — she eliminates it. Not the people. The inefficiency itself. She restructures your role, your shift, your route, your meals, your sleep schedule, until you are performing at peak output.

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: I used to paint. Watercolors. Landscapes. She determined it was non-productive recreational activity and reassigned my free hours to supplementary training modules.
